text:
User: not rpc_who; that's too narrow -- it should be set to whatever sets the agent identity, not just `who` [...] Assistant: The right choke point is `resolve_session_inner` (`src/daemon/server/resolution.rs:107`), not `rpc_who`. Its terminal failure branches (lines 161-166 for `Strict`, 199-206 for `Channel`) are where every RPC handler that resolves caller identity — `who`, `chat_write`, `chat_read`, `propose`, `channels_create/edit/join/leave/switch`, `pty_send/attach`, `turn_start/check/end`, `invite`, `channel_add_member` (11 call sites total) — currently bails with "no active session, run session-start."

Registration itself is already centralized too: `rpc_session_start` (`session_start.rs:35`) → `Store::register_session` (`sessions.rs:93-150`), which today only fires from the harness's own SessionStart hook, with `agent_slug` taken verbatim from the hook payload — nothing server-side ever synthesizes it.

So the fix is one bridge: when `resolve_session_inner` hits its failure branch and no alias resolves, instead of bailing, synthesize an `agent_slug` (`<clientinfo-or-harness-name>/<random-suffix>`) and call `Store::register_session` before re-resolving — every caller in that list gets JIT identity for free, with no per-handler duplication.
